text: and she didn’t know what was happening . “You were right,” Cooper said, jarring Lara from her thoughts . “About what?” she asked. His eyes were on the shacks, on the filth and waste piled around them . “We need to help these people. I can’t… Seeing them now, how they suffered…” He shook his head and met her gaze. “How you suffered. I understand your anger .” Lara nodded. They were helping. That was more than she’d expected, when she first met the base’s inhabitants . The gate into the Market was open, as it normally was, but the activity beyond was unusual for this time of day. The rising sun cast long shadows over everything, including the cluster of bots gathered amidst the stalls . Several gearheads, touting guns, hurried through the crowd, pushing toward the Bot District. The door guard from Kitty’s clambered behind them . Cooper brought the humans to a stop and waited until the gearheads were out of sight before he proceeded forward . Greene stood at his stand, steam rising from his cooking food as though nothing unusual were happening. Some of the other bots — mostly the ones that didn’t look human — continued their normal routines, as well, seemingly unaffected by the commotion and confusion . The gate between the Market and the Bot District was open. The pair of gearheads on guard stared northeast, toward the Clinic, ignoring the bots streaming out of the District . More gunshots boomed from within the wall, each one a jolt to Lara’s heart . “Chester, stay with Newton and Lara,” Cooper ordered. “The rest of you, take up positions around the perimeter of the crowd. We’re the only defense any of these people have right now. Do not hesitate to fire on the gearheads at the gate if they make a move .” As the humans entered the Market, clumping together to form a group separate from the gathered bots, the soldiers dispersed. Lara looked over her people; they were a bedraggled bunch, malnourished and rough, most carrying makeshift weaponry — lengths of old piping, sharpened metal shards with cloth wrapped around one end, wooden planks. Gary stood at the front, bloody knife in hand . The weapons would be useless against the gearheads, but if the people were willing to fight, and if they could convince the other bots to help … Lara pointed to an empty stall. It was just a wooden board set across several stacks of cinderblocks, but it would do. “There, Newton. Get their attention .” “Not exactly conventional, but I suppose that convention is the least of our concerns in this situation…unless it is the defiance of the current — ” “I have no idea what the hell you’re talking about, but I sure as shit hope they will. Get to it .” “Right.” He walked to the stand. “Things were simpler by far when I was just a computer program .” “Stay close to me,” Chester said, positioning himself in front of Lara with his back to her and Newton . With surprising grace, Newton climbed atop the board. His shoulders sagged for a few moments before he straightened, and, finally, turned to face the crowd . “Bots, humans, synthetics, lend me your ears !” Lara arched a brow. This was on him, now. As long as he convinced the bots to fight alongside the humans, she didn’t care what he said, or how weird it sounded . Many of the bots shifted their focus to Newton, some with questioning looks on their faces — not that any of their expressions were easy to read. Except Greene’s. Blank was a simple one . “I suppose I understand how the reference would be lost on you,” Newton said to himself. He released a short burst of static, and Lara imagined that he’d cleared his throat. “I doubt that any of you know my face. I hardly know it, myself. But as I look out, I recognize the faces of every bot here . “It has been many years since I traveled this world — the Dust, as you call it — and done my work. Many years since most of you awoke …” “I know that voice,” said one of the bots, stepping forward. “But it can’t be .” “The Prophet,” another offered . Murmurs of agreement rose from the bots. Lara glanced toward the gate. The two guards had been joined by two more gearheads, all of them staring at Newton . Shit . “That is what many of you took to calling me, yes, but my name is Newton. One hundred and ninety-seven years ago, I was sent out into the wasteland, tasked with repairing and reactivating all the bots I could find. The goal was to rebuild the world, hand-in-hand with humanity .” “You saying you turned all these things on?” one of the humans demanded . They’d been sparked to anger now, had tasted blood; their volatility pulsed from them in waves . “Listen to what he has to say,” Lara shouted over the rising voices. “He’s not the one responsible for this place .” “No,” Newton said, loud but gentle. “Your anger is understandable. Justified. I blame myself in no small part for the state this world is in, Cheyenne being no exception. I hid from what I perceived as my failure for decades. Not far from here, in fact. Never truly knowing what was happening here, never knowing what all of you have been forced to endure . “Because of the courage of Lara Brooks, I chose to leave hiding. To come here and right the wrongs that have been done to all of you.” He turned his head to fully face the bots . The gearheads approached. As they drew closer, Lara recognized the one in front. He was part of the group that followed her and Ronin out of Cheyenne. The one who shot Ronin. She dropped her hand
